frameworks.name: psychophysical reverse correlation
frameworks.one_line: stimulus-fluctuation kernels relating noisy input to choice behavior

frameworks.name: bounded evidence accumulation / decision-making models
frameworks.one_line: decision bound and integration mechanism shape observed kernels

frameworks.name: linear sensory filter model
frameworks.one_line: assumption that kernels recover pure spatiotemporal sensory weights

core_question: Do psychophysical reverse-correlation kernels reflect only sensory filters, or are they also shaped by decision-making processes?
key_assumptions: behavior arises from sensory encoding followed by a decision stage
stimulus fluctuations across trials drive choice variability
decision bound and evidence integration can be modeled within accumulator frameworks
manipulations: decision bound height in simulated accumulator models
mechanism of evidence integration (e.g., leaky vs perfect)
trial-to-trial variability of sensory/motor delays and decision times
key_findings: reverse correlations deviate systematically from the true sensory filter
changes in decision bound and integration mechanism alter the observed kernel
variability in sensory/motor delays and decision times distorts kernels in ways not attributable to sensory mechanisms
interpretations: psychophysical kernels jointly index sensory and decision-making processes
with proper decision-stage modeling, reverse correlation becomes a tool for studying both
